constraints_observed: No canonical requirement or plan files modified
No local build or test commands executed
Only this bounded audit receipt was written

findings.summary: The backend still declares MyBatis-Plus, sqlite-jdbc, and the file-backed Spring AI vector-store module, with no Spring Data MongoDB starter.
findings.recommendation: Replace MyBatis-Plus and sqlite-jdbc with spring-boot-starter-data-mongodb. If vector data is in migration scope, select a MongoDB-compatible Spring AI vector-store dependency supported by the pinned Spring AI 1.1.4 BOM; do not retain SimpleVectorStore as a production persistence fallback.

findings.summary: Default configuration still points to jdbc:sqlite:dbdata/xianyu_assistant.db, persists vectors to dbdata/vectorstore.json, and configures MyBatis type handlers.
findings.recommendation: Use spring.data.mongodb.uri=${SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_URI:mongodb://localhost:27017/xianyu_assistant} as the single authoritative connection setting. If a separate database property is retained, use spring.data.mongodb.database=${SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_DATABASE:xianyu_assistant} and ensure URI/database precedence is documented. Remove datasource, mybatis-plus, and SimpleVectorStore file-path settings after their consumers are migrated.

findings.summary: The runtime image creates /app/dbdata and deployment examples mount it as the SQLite/vector database volume. The image exposes no MongoDB connection contract or health check.
findings.recommendation: Stop presenting /app/dbdata as a database volume. Keep only genuinely local transient/log paths. Document and pass SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_URI (and optionally SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_DATABASE). Provide a Docker Compose example with an application service depending on a mongo service, a named /data/db volume, a MongoDB healthcheck, and credentials supplied via environment or secrets rather than baked into the image.

findings.summary: The JAR installer only accepts port and JVM options, creates local data/log directories, and starts the application without MongoDB URI or database configuration. A migrated application would fail or accidentally use an unsafe default when installed this way.
findings.recommendation: Require or interactively collect MONGODB_URI (recommended external name) and export it to Spring as SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_URI before launching. Optionally accept MONGODB_DATABASE. Validate that the URI is non-empty without printing credentials, persist settings in a permission-restricted environment file or service definition, and update restart instructions so later launches retain them.

findings.summary: The only backend delivery gate is a multi-platform Docker build whose Dockerfile packages with -DskipTests. There is no Maven test job, MongoDB service container, Mongo-backed application context check, or post-build container startup check.
findings.recommendation: Add a backend verification job on ubuntu-latest with a MongoDB service (pinned major version), health options using mongosh ping, SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_URI targeting the service, JDK 21 setup, and ./mvnw test (or verify). Add at least one context/repository integration test that creates indexes and performs CRUD against the service. Make the Docker publish job depend on this job. Retain docker/build-push-action for image verification and optionally load a single amd64 image in a non-publish smoke job, run it against MongoDB, and poll an application health endpoint. All build/test execution must remain in Actions.

findings.summary: User and technical documentation consistently describes SQLite, MyBatis-Plus, SQL schema initialization, dbdata persistence, and file-backed vectors as current architecture and operational requirements.
findings.recommendation: Update installation, architecture, persistence, operations, backup/restore, scaling, monitoring, and testing sections together. Remove SQL schema initialization instructions once code is removed. State MongoDB version/edition requirements, URI examples with redacted credentials, database/index bootstrap behavior, backup/restore semantics, and whether Atlas is mandatory for vector search. Regenerate source-index documentation after migration so generated MyBatis/SQLite references do not remain authoritative.

findings.summary: The vector store is definitely local persistent business data. Log ZIP creation is operational/transient, while image upload bytes are forwarded to the Goofish CDN and do not appear to be retained locally.
findings.recommendation: Migrate vector persistence to the selected MongoDB vector implementation. Do not move logs or temporary ZIP files into MongoDB solely to satisfy the migration wording. GridFS is only needed if a later audit finds application-owned binary objects retained locally; the current image upload path alone does not justify it.

runtime_environment: SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_URI (required in production; includes hosts, credentials, replica set/TLS options, and may include database)
SPRING_DATA_MONGODB_DATABASE (optional only when database is not encoded in the URI)
Do not log the URI or store credentials in repository files
ci_gates: Maven test/verify executes on GitHub Actions with MongoDB URI injected
Mongo repository/index CRUD integration coverage passes
Docker image builds only after backend verification
Optional container smoke test reaches application health endpoint while connected to MongoDB
Static residual scan rejects sqlite-jdbc, mybatis-plus, jdbc:sqlite, spring.datasource, dbdata/xianyu_assistant.db, and vectorstore.json in production code/config/deployment docs

verification.method: read-only UTF-8 PowerShell inspection and rg residual audit
verification.completion_claim_allowed: Audit findings are complete; full MongoDB migration completion is not claimable until GitHub Actions provides successful build, test, and container evidence.

governance_notes: The installed vibe SKILL.md references protocols/runtime.md and protocols/review.md, but those files were absent at the referenced location; the main skill contract and root delegation envelope were used as authority.
No specialist dispatch was needed for this bounded read-only delivery audit.
